Yours is batch_size -> likely Option.__init__ new kwarg plus processing in Parameter/Command.invoke or wherever batching happens. Proposal: I'll only touch Option.get_default/default-related code and add a small hook in Command.main(); please avoid those spots and add your batch_size code in separate methods/params. I'll also take types.py fully (need it for dynamic-default type validation) - do you actually need to edit types.py, or just core.py? Let's each finish our edits, then diff-review before finalizing to make sure we didn't collide.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Good news: I don't actually need Option.__init__ or to_info_dict at all - dynamic defaults just reuse the existing self.default attribute (already set generically by Parameter.__init__), no new stored state needed. So let's split Option class by METHOD instead of by file: YOU keep __init__ (lines ~2533-2664), to_info_dict (~2665-2677), and your new batch-iteration method (please add it right after to_info_dict, around line 2678, before get_error_hint). I will only touch: get_help_extra (~2822-2900, adding a dict-default branch next to the existing 'elif inspect.isfunction(default_value)' dynamic-default display line) and get_default (~2912-2926, so a dict default returns None here instead of leaking the raw dict as the value). I'll also add one new method of my own right after get_default (~2927) for resolving the dynamic value, plus edit Parameter.__init__ (~2076-2170, base class, you said you don't need this) for validating dict-default shape, plus Command.main (~1295-1408) for the second-pass resolution loop. This should keep our diffs on non-overlapping line ranges even within the same file/class. Does this work for you?
